after.title: Functional test: Import to different project
after.description:
## Objective
Test importing exported tasks into a different project directory.

## Test Procedure
1. Create a test project:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /tmp/test-import-project
   cd /tmp/test-import-project
   cleo init
   ```

2. Export a task from claude-todo:
   ```bash
   cd /mnt/projects/claude-todo
   cleo export-tasks T1329 --output /tmp/test-single-export.json
   ```

3. Import into test project:
   ```bash
   cd /tmp/test-import-project
   cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-single-export.json
   ```

4. Verify import succeeded

## Expected Results
- New task created in test project with NEW ID (not T1329)
- Task title, description, status preserved
- Import logged in todo-log.json

## Verification
```bash
cd /tmp/test-import-project
cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length'  # Should be 1
cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks[0].title'  # Should match original
```

## Cleanup
```bash
rm -rf /tmp/test-import-project
```

before.title: Functional test: Import with ID conflicts
before.description: Test importing into project that already has overlapping task IDs, verify automatic remapping works correctly

after.title: Functional test: Import with ID conflicts
after.description:
## Objective
Test that importing into a project with existing IDs correctly remaps to avoid conflicts.

## Test Procedure
1. Create test project with existing tasks:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /tmp/test-conflict-project
   cd /tmp/test-conflict-project
   cleo init
   cleo add "Existing Task 1"  # Creates T001
   cleo add "Existing Task 2"  # Creates T002
   ```

2. Export tasks with IDs T001, T002 from claude-todo (or create export with those IDs)

3. Import - IDs should be remapped:
   ```bash
   cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-conflict-export.json
   ```

## Expected Results
- Imported tasks get NEW IDs (T003, T004, etc.)
- Original T001, T002 unchanged
- parentId/depends references updated to new IDs

## Verification
```bash
# Check no duplicate IDs
cleo list --format json | jq '[.tasks[].id] | unique | length'
# Should equal total task count

# Check imported tasks have different IDs
cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks[] | select(.title | contains("Revert")) | .id'
# Should NOT be T1329
```

## Cleanup
```bash
rm -rf /tmp/test-conflict-project
```

before.title: Functional test: Dry-run mode verification
before.description: Test --dry-run mode for both export and import, verify no actual changes made while preview is accurate

after.title: Functional test: Dry-run mode verification
after.description:
## Objective
Verify --dry-run mode shows preview without making changes.

## Test Procedure

### Export Dry-Run
```bash
cleo export-tasks T1329 --dry-run
```
- Should show what would be exported
- No file created (unless --output specified)

### Import Dry-Run
```bash
cd /tmp/test-import-project
cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-export.json --dry-run
```
- Should show ID remapping preview
- Should show what tasks would be created
- todo.json should NOT be modified

## Verification
```bash
# Before import dry-run
BEFORE=$(cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length')

# Run dry-run
cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-export.json --dry-run

# After import dry-run  
AFTER=$(cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length')

# Should be equal
[[ "$BEFORE" == "$AFTER" ]] && echo "PASS: No changes made"
```

## Expected Output
Dry-run should display:
- Tasks that would be imported
- ID mappings (source → target)
- Any conflicts detected
- Summary statistics

before.title: Functional test: Audit logging verification
before.description: Verify import operations create proper audit log entries with provenance metadata

after.title: Functional test: Audit logging verification
after.description:
## Objective
Verify import operations create proper audit log entries with provenance metadata.

## Test Procedure
1. Create test project and import tasks
2. Check todo-log.json for import entries

## Expected Log Entry Structure
```json
{
  "operation": "import",
  "timestamp": "...",
  "taskId": "T001",
  "details": {
    "sourceFile": "/path/to/export.json",
    "sourceProject": "original-project",
    "originalId": "T042",
    "idRemap": {"T042": "T001"},
    "options": {...}
  }
}
```

## Verification Commands
```bash
cd /tmp/test-import-project

# Check log exists
cat .cleo/todo-log.json | jq '.entries | last'

# Verify import operation logged
cat .cleo/todo-log.json | jq '.entries[] | select(.operation == "import")'

# Check provenance metadata
cat .cleo/todo-log.json | jq '.entries[] | select(.operation == "import") | .details.sourceProject'
```

## Checklist
- [ ] Import creates log entry
- [ ] operation == 'import'
- [ ] sourceFile recorded
- [ ] sourceProject recorded  
- [ ] originalId (pre-remap) recorded
- [ ] idRemap table recorded

before.title: Revert manual CLAUDE.md edit
before.description:
## Objective
Revert the manual edit made to CLAUDE.md that violated the documentation workflow.

## Background
Per docs/DOCUMENTATION-MAINTENANCE.md, CLAUDE.md should ONLY be updated via `cleo init --update-claude-md` which injects content from templates/CLAUDE-INJECTION.md. A manual edit was made adding an 'Export/Import (Cross-Project)' section directly to CLAUDE.md.

## Instructions
1. Read CLAUDE.md and locate the manually added section (search for 'Export/Import')
2. The section starts with '### Export/Import (Cross-Project)' and ends before '### Command Discovery'
3. Remove this entire section (approximately 15 lines)
4. Do NOT add anything - T1330 will update the injection template, T1335 will re-inject

## Verification
- CLAUDE.md should not contain 'export-tasks' or 'import-tasks' after this task
- The section '### Export/Import (Cross-Project)' should not exist
- Run: grep -c 'export-tasks' CLAUDE.md (should return 0)

## Files to Modify
- CLAUDE.md (remove the manually added section)
acceptance: Manual export/import section removed from CLAUDE.md
No other changes made to CLAUDE.md

before.title: Run cleo init --update-claude-md
before.description:
## Objective
Run the official injection command to update CLAUDE.md from the template.

## Prerequisites
- T1330 must be complete (CLAUDE-INJECTION.md updated with export/import)
- T1329 should be complete (manual edit reverted)

## Instructions
1. Verify templates/CLAUDE-INJECTION.md contains export/import section
2. Run: cleo init --update-claude-md
3. Verify CLAUDE.md now contains the export/import section

## Command
```bash
cleo init --update-claude-md
```

## What This Does
- Reads templates/CLAUDE-INJECTION.md
- Replaces content between <!-- CLEO:START --> and <!-- CLEO:END --> in CLAUDE.md
- This is the ONLY approved way to update CLAUDE.md per DOCUMENTATION-MAINTENANCE.md

## Verification
- CLAUDE.md contains export-tasks commands
- CLAUDE.md contains import-tasks commands
- grep 'export-tasks' CLAUDE.md returns matches
- The <!-- CLEO:START --> tag shows updated version

before.title: Update CLAUDE-INJECTION.md with export/import commands
before.description:
## Objective
Add export-tasks and import-tasks commands to the CLAUDE-INJECTION.md template so they get properly injected into project CLAUDE.md files.

## Background
Per docs/DOCUMENTATION-MAINTENANCE.md, Layer 1 (CLAUDE-INJECTION.md) should contain only essential commands (max 10 total). Export/import commands are essential for cross-project task portability.

## Instructions
1. Read templates/CLAUDE-INJECTION.md to understand current structure
2. Find the '### Essential Commands' section
3. Add a new subsection '### Export/Import (Cross-Project)' after Essential Commands
4. Add the following content:

```markdown
### Export/Import (Cross-Project)
```bash
# Export tasks to another project
ct export-tasks T001 --output task.json            # Export single task
ct export-tasks T001 --subtree --output epic.json  # Export epic with children

# Import tasks from another project
ct import-tasks task.json                          # Import into current project
ct import-tasks task.json --dry-run                # Preview import
ct import-tasks task.json --parent T050            # Import as children of T050
```

Full docs: `docs/export-import.md`
```

## Reference Files
- docs/export-import.md (user documentation created in Phase 5)
- scripts/export-tasks.sh (for command flags reference)
- scripts/import-tasks.sh (for command flags reference)

## Verification
- templates/CLAUDE-INJECTION.md contains export-tasks and import-tasks
- Section is concise (Layer 1 = minimal)
- Points to docs/export-import.md for full documentation

before.description:
## Objective
Functionally test exporting a single task from the real cleo project.

## Test Procedure
1. Choose a simple task to export (e.g., a completed subtask)
2. Run export command:
   ```bash
   cleo export-tasks T1329 --output /tmp/test-export-single.json
   ```
3. Verify output file structure

## Expected Output Structure
```json
{
  "_meta": {
    "format": "cleo-export",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "exportedAt": "...",
    "sourceProject": "claude-todo",
    "stats": {...},

before.title: Functional test: Dry-run mode verification
before.description:
## Objective
Verify --dry-run mode shows preview without making changes.

## Test Procedure

### Export Dry-Run
```bash
cleo export-tasks T1329 --dry-run
```
- Should show what would be exported
- No file created (unless --output specified)

### Import Dry-Run
```bash
cd /tmp/test-import-project
cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-export.json --dry-run
```
- Should show ID remapping preview
- Should show what tasks would be created
- todo.json should NOT be modified

## Verification
```bash
# Before import dry-run
BEFORE=$(cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length')

# Run dry-run
cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-export.json --dry-run

# After import dry-run  
AFTER=$(cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length')

# Should be equal
[[ "$BEFORE" == "$AFTER" ]] && echo "PASS: No changes made"
```

## Expected Output
Dry-run should display:
- Tasks that would be imported
- ID mappings (source → target)
- Any conflicts detected
- Summary statistics

before.title: Research: Map all session-aware commands and their output paths
before.description: Identify all commands that should trigger context alerts. Map their output functions (output_success, output_json, etc.) to understand injection points. Document current stderr vs stdout patterns.

after.title: Research: Map all session-aware commands and their output paths
after.description:
GOAL: Identify all commands that should trigger context alerts and understand their output patterns.

STEPS:
1. List all session-aware commands in scripts/ directory
2. For each command, identify:
   - Does it check for active session?
   - What output functions does it use (output_success, output_json, etc)?
   - Where does stderr vs stdout go?
3. Create a markdown table documenting findings
4. Recommend which commands should trigger alerts (write operations that indicate work progress)

OUTPUT: Create file docs/research/context-alert-triggers.md with findings

ACCEPTANCE:
- All write commands analyzed (add, complete, update, focus, session)
- Output function patterns documented
- Recommended trigger points identified

before.title: Run cleo init --update-claude-md
before.description:
## Objective
Run the official injection command to update CLAUDE.md from the template.

## Prerequisites
- T1330 must be complete (CLAUDE-INJECTION.md updated with export/import)
- T1329 should be complete (manual edit reverted)

## Instructions
1. Verify templates/CLAUDE-INJECTION.md contains export/import section
2. Run: cleo init --update-claude-md
3. Verify CLAUDE.md now contains the export/import section

## Command
```bash
cleo init --update-claude-md
```

## What This Does
- Reads templates/CLAUDE-INJECTION.md
- Replaces content between <!-- CLEO:START --> and <!-- CLEO:END --> in CLAUDE.md
- This is the ONLY approved way to update CLAUDE.md per DOCUMENTATION-MAINTENANCE.md

## Verification
- CLAUDE.md contains export-tasks commands
- CLAUDE.md contains import-tasks commands
- grep 'export-tasks' CLAUDE.md returns matches
- The <!-- CLEO:START --> tag shows updated version

before.title: Design: Context alert library (lib/context-alert.sh)
before.description: Design new library with: check_and_alert_context() function, session-awareness (skip if no active session), threshold-crossing detection (only alert on status change), stderr output with attention-grabbing format (emoji, box, uppercase), configurable enable/disable.

after.title: Design: Context alert library (lib/context-alert.sh)
after.description:
GOAL: Design the context alert library API and behavior before implementation.

DEPENDS ON: T1321 (research findings)

DESIGN DECISIONS TO MAKE:
1. Function signature: check_and_alert_context() parameters
2. Session detection: How to check if session is active
3. Threshold tracking: How to detect status CHANGE (not just current status)
4. Output format: Box style, emoji, colors for TTY

DESIGN DOCUMENT STRUCTURE:
```
## API
- check_and_alert_context [--force]
- Returns: 0 if no alert needed, 1 if alert emitted

## Session Detection
- Check .cleo/.current-session file exists
- If no session, silently return 0

## Threshold Crossing Detection
- Read current status from .context-state.json
- Read last-alerted status from .context-last-alert
- Only alert if status > last-alerted
- Update last-alerted after emitting alert

## Output Format (stderr)
⚠️ ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   CONTEXT [STATUS]: XX% (CURRENT/MAX tokens)
   Action: [recommended action based on status]
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ ⚠️
```

OUTPUT: Create file docs/design/context-alert-library.md

before.title: Functional test: Import with ID conflicts
before.description:
## Objective
Test that importing into a project with existing IDs correctly remaps to avoid conflicts.

## Test Procedure
1. Create test project with existing tasks:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /tmp/test-conflict-project
   cd /tmp/test-conflict-project
   cleo init
   cleo add "Existing Task 1"  # Creates T001
   cleo add "Existing Task 2"  # Creates T002
   ```

2. Export tasks with IDs T001, T002 from claude-todo (or create export with those IDs)

3. Import - IDs should be remapped:
   ```bash
   cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-conflict-export.json
   ```

## Expected Results
- Imported tasks get NEW IDs (T003, T004, etc.)
- Original T001, T002 unchanged
- parentId/depends references updated to new IDs

## Verification
```bash
# Check no duplicate IDs
cleo list --format json | jq '[.tasks[].id] | unique | length'
# Should equal total task count

# Check imported tasks have different IDs
cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks[] | select(.title | contains("Revert")) | .id'
# Should NOT be T1329
```

## Cleanup
```bash
rm -rf /tmp/test-conflict-project
```

before.description:
## Objective
Add export-tasks and import-tasks to COMMANDS-INDEX.json registry.

## Instructions

### 1. Add to categories.sync array
Add 'export-tasks' and 'import-tasks' (alphabetical order)

### 2. Add export-tasks command entry to commands array:
- name: export-tasks, script: export-tasks.sh, category: sync
- doc: export-tasks.md, agentRelevance: medium
- flags: --format, --output, --subtree, --filter, --include-deps, --dry-run, --interactive
- exitCodes: [0, 2, 3, 4], jsonOutput: true
- synopsis: Export tasks to portable JSON package for cross-project transfer

### 3. Add import-tasks command entry:
- name: import-tasks, script: import-tasks.sh, category: sync
- doc: import-tasks.md, agentRelevance: medium
- flags: --format, --dry-run, --parent, --on-duplicate, --on-missing-dep, --on-phase-mismatch, --add-label
- exitCodes: [0, 2, 3, 4, 6], jsonOutput: true
- synopsis: Import tasks from export package with ID remapping and conflict resolution

### 4. Update _meta.totalCommands: 41 → 43

### 5. Add to quickLookup: exportTasks → export-tasks, importTasks → import-tasks

## Verification
- jq . docs/commands/COMMANDS-INDEX.json succeeds
- cleo commands export-tasks returns entry
- cleo commands import-tasks returns entry

before.title: Integrate: Add context alerts to complete, focus, add, session commands
before.description: Source context-alert.sh in target commands. Call check_and_alert_context() after successful operations. Commands: complete-task.sh, focus.sh (set subcommand), add-task.sh, session.sh (start/end). Ensure alert appears BEFORE JSON output.

after.title: Integrate: Add context alerts to complete, focus, add, session commands
after.description:
GOAL: Integrate context-alert.sh into target commands so alerts fire automatically.

DEPENDS ON: T1323 (library implementation)

COMMANDS TO MODIFY:
1. scripts/complete-task.sh - Alert after successful completion
2. scripts/focus.sh - Alert after 'set' subcommand
3. scripts/add-task.sh - Alert after successful task creation
4. scripts/session.sh - Alert after 'start' and 'end' subcommands

INTEGRATION PATTERN FOR EACH COMMAND:
```bash
# At top of script, after other sources
[[ -f "$LIB_DIR/context-alert.sh" ]] && source "$LIB_DIR/context-alert.sh"

# After successful operation, BEFORE outputting JSON
# (so alert appears first, then JSON response)
if [[ "$success" == true ]]; then
    check_and_alert_context 2>/dev/null || true  # Don't fail on alert errors
fi

# Then output JSON response
output_json ...
```

CRITICAL: Alert must appear BEFORE JSON output so agent sees it first.

TESTING:
1. Start a session
2. Create mock .context-state.json with warning status
3. Run each command and verify alert appears before JSON
acceptance: Alert appears BEFORE JSON in all commands

after.title: Config: Add context alert settings to config schema
after.description:
GOAL: Add configuration options for context alerts to config schema.

DEPENDS ON: T1323 (so we know what needs to be configurable)

CONFIG OPTIONS TO ADD:
```json
{
  "contextAlerts": {
    "enabled": true,
    "minThreshold": "warning",
    "triggerCommands": ["complete", "focus", "add", "session"],
    "suppressDuration": 0
  }
}
```

IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:
1. Update schemas/config.schema.json with contextAlerts object
2. Update templates/config.template.json with defaults
3. Update lib/context-alert.sh to read config:
   - Check contextAlerts.enabled before alerting
   - Check minThreshold to filter low-priority alerts
   - Check if current command is in triggerCommands
4. Update docs/commands/config.md with new options

CONFIG READING PATTERN:
```bash
# In context-alert.sh
alerts_enabled=$(jq -r '.contextAlerts.enabled // true' "$CONFIG_FILE")
if [[ "$alerts_enabled" != "true" ]]; then
    return 0
fi
```

before.description: Update AGENT-INJECTION.md, TODO_Task_Management.md with info about automatic alerts. Document config options. Add to context.md related section.

after.title: Docs: Document automatic context alerts in agent guides
after.description:
GOAL: Document automatic context alerts in all relevant documentation.

DEPENDS ON: T1324 (feature complete), T1325 (config complete)

DOCUMENTATION UPDATES:

1. docs/commands/context.md - Add section:
```markdown
## Automatic Alerts

When a CLEO session is active, context alerts trigger automatically after:
- Task completion (`cleo complete`)
- Focus changes (`cleo focus set`)
- Task creation (`cleo add`)
- Session start/end

Alerts appear on stderr BEFORE command output, ensuring visibility.

### Alert Format
⚠️ ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   CONTEXT WARNING: 78% (156000/200000 tokens)
   Action: Consider wrapping up current work
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ ⚠️

### Configuration
See `cleo config` for contextAlerts settings.
```

2. templates/AGENT-INJECTION.md - Update Context Monitoring section:
   - Add note about automatic alerts
   - Explain they appear before JSON

3. docs/TODO_Task_Management.md - Add to Context Monitoring section:
   - Brief mention of automatic alerts during session
   - Link to context.md for details

4. Create docs/guides/context-safeguard.md if not exists:
   - Full guide on context safeguard system
   - Setup instructions
   - Automatic alerts explanation

before.title: Functional test: Dry-run mode verification
before.description:
## Objective
Verify --dry-run mode shows preview without making changes.

## Test Procedure

### Export Dry-Run
```bash
cleo export-tasks T1329 --dry-run
```
- Should show what would be exported
- No file created (unless --output specified)

### Import Dry-Run
```bash
cd /tmp/test-import-project
cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-export.json --dry-run
```
- Should show ID remapping preview
- Should show what tasks would be created
- todo.json should NOT be modified

## Verification
```bash
# Before import dry-run
BEFORE=$(cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length')

# Run dry-run
cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-export.json --dry-run

# After import dry-run  
AFTER=$(cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length')

# Should be equal
[[ "$BEFORE" == "$AFTER" ]] && echo "PASS: No changes made"
```

## Expected Output
Dry-run should display:
- Tasks that would be imported
- ID mappings (source → target)
- Any conflicts detected
- Summary statistics

before.title: Functional test: Import to different project
before.description:
## Objective
Test importing exported tasks into a different project directory.

## Test Procedure
1. Create a test project:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /tmp/test-import-project
   cd /tmp/test-import-project
   cleo init
   ```

2. Export a task from claude-todo:
   ```bash
   cd /mnt/projects/claude-todo
   cleo export-tasks T1329 --output /tmp/test-single-export.json
   ```

3. Import into test project:
   ```bash
   cd /tmp/test-import-project
   cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-single-export.json
   ```

4. Verify import succeeded

## Expected Results
- New task created in test project with NEW ID (not T1329)
- Task title, description, status preserved
- Import logged in todo-log.json

## Verification
```bash
cd /tmp/test-import-project
cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length'  # Should be 1
cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks[0].title'  # Should match original
```

## Cleanup
```bash
rm -rf /tmp/test-import-project
```

before.title: Research: Map all session-aware commands and their output paths
before.description:
GOAL: Identify all commands that should trigger context alerts and understand their output patterns.

STEPS:
1. List all session-aware commands in scripts/ directory
2. For each command, identify:
   - Does it check for active session?
   - What output functions does it use (output_success, output_json, etc)?
   - Where does stderr vs stdout go?
3. Create a markdown table documenting findings
4. Recommend which commands should trigger alerts (write operations that indicate work progress)

OUTPUT: Create file docs/research/context-alert-triggers.md with findings

ACCEPTANCE:
- All write commands analyzed (add, complete, update, focus, session)
- Output function patterns documented
- Recommended trigger points identified

## Objective
Verify import operations create proper audit log entries with provenance metadata.

## Test Procedure
1. Create test project and import tasks
2. Check todo-log.json for import entries

## Expected Log Entry Structure
```json
{
  "operation": "import",
  "timestamp": "...",
  "taskId": "T001",
  "details": {
    "sourceFile": "/path/to/export.json",
    "sourceProject": "original-project",
    "originalId": "T042",
    "idRemap": {"T042": "T001"},
    "options": {...}
  }
}
```

## Verification Commands
```bash

# Check provenance metadata
cat .cleo/todo-log.json | jq '.entries[] | select(.operation == "import") | .details.sourceProject'
```

## Checklist
- [ ] Import creates log entry
- [ ] operation == 'import'
- [ ] sourceFile recorded
- [ ] sourceProject recorded  
- [ ] originalId (pre-remap) recorded
- [ ] idRemap table recorded

before.title: Design: Context alert library (lib/context-alert.sh)
before.description:
GOAL: Design the context alert library API and behavior before implementation.

DEPENDS ON: T1321 (research findings)

DESIGN DECISIONS TO MAKE:
1. Function signature: check_and_alert_context() parameters
2. Session detection: How to check if session is active
3. Threshold tracking: How to detect status CHANGE (not just current status)
4. Output format: Box style, emoji, colors for TTY

DESIGN DOCUMENT STRUCTURE:
```
## API
- check_and_alert_context [--force]
- Returns: 0 if no alert needed, 1 if alert emitted

## Session Detection
- Check .cleo/.current-session file exists
- If no session, silently return 0

## Threshold Crossing Detection
- Read current status from .context-state.json
- Read last-alerted status from .context-last-alert
- Only alert if status > last-alerted
- Update last-alerted after emitting alert

## Output Format (stderr)
⚠️ ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   CONTEXT [STATUS]: XX% (CURRENT/MAX tokens)
   Action: [recommended action based on status]
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ ⚠️
```

OUTPUT: Create file docs/design/context-alert-library.md

CONFIG OPTIONS TO ADD:
```json
{
  "contextAlerts": {
    "enabled": true,
    "minThreshold": "warning",
    "triggerCommands": ["complete", "focus", "add", "session"],
    "suppressDuration": 0
  }
}
```

IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:
1. Update schemas/config.schema.json with contextAlerts object
2. Update templates/config.template.json with defaults
3. Update lib/context-alert.sh to read config:
   - Check contextAlerts.enabled before alerting
   - Check minThreshold to filter low-priority alerts
   - Check if current command is in triggerCommands
4. Update docs/commands/config.md with new options

CONFIG READING PATTERN:
```bash
# In context-alert.sh
alerts_enabled=$(jq -r '.contextAlerts.enabled // true' "$CONFIG_FILE")
if [[ "$alerts_enabled" != "true" ]]; then
    return 0
fi
```

before.title: Docs: Document automatic context alerts in agent guides
before.description:
GOAL: Document automatic context alerts in all relevant documentation.

DEPENDS ON: T1324 (feature complete), T1325 (config complete)

DOCUMENTATION UPDATES:

1. docs/commands/context.md - Add section:
```markdown
## Automatic Alerts

When a CLEO session is active, context alerts trigger automatically after:
- Task completion (`cleo complete`)
- Focus changes (`cleo focus set`)
- Task creation (`cleo add`)
- Session start/end

Alerts appear on stderr BEFORE command output, ensuring visibility.

### Alert Format
⚠️ ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   CONTEXT WARNING: 78% (156000/200000 tokens)
   Action: Consider wrapping up current work
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ ⚠️

### Configuration
See `cleo config` for contextAlerts settings.
```

2. templates/AGENT-INJECTION.md - Update Context Monitoring section:
   - Add note about automatic alerts
   - Explain they appear before JSON

3. docs/TODO_Task_Management.md - Add to Context Monitoring section:
   - Brief mention of automatic alerts during session
   - Link to context.md for details

4. Create docs/guides/context-safeguard.md if not exists:
   - Full guide on context safeguard system
   - Setup instructions
   - Automatic alerts explanation

before.title: Functional test: Export subtree (epic with children)
before.description:
## Objective
Functionally test exporting an epic with its full subtree (children and grandchildren).

## Test Procedure
1. Export Phase 6 epic (T1328) with --subtree:
   ```bash
   cleo export-tasks T1328 --subtree --output /tmp/test-export-subtree.json
   ```
2. Verify all children are included
3. Verify hierarchy is preserved

## Expected Results
- T1328 (parent) + all 13 subtasks = 14 tasks total
- parentId relationships preserved
- depends arrays preserved

## Verification Commands
```bash
# Check task count
jq '.tasks | length' /tmp/test-export-subtree.json  # Should be 14

# Check parent task present
jq '.tasks[] | select(.id == "T1328")' /tmp/test-export-subtree.json

# Check hierarchy preserved
jq '.tasks[] | select(.parentId == "T1328") | .id' /tmp/test-export-subtree.json
```

## Verification Checklist
- [ ] Command exits 0
- [ ] Contains parent + all children (14 tasks)
- [ ] parentId values preserved
- [ ] depends arrays preserved
before.blockedBy: export-tasks.sh stub functions shadow lib/export.sh. Need to remove stubs and properly integrate library functions before testing can proceed.

before.title: Functional test: Import to different project
before.description:
## Objective
Test importing exported tasks into a different project directory.

## Test Procedure
1. Create a test project:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /tmp/test-import-project
   cd /tmp/test-import-project
   cleo init
   ```

2. Export a task from claude-todo:
   ```bash
   cd /mnt/projects/claude-todo
   cleo export-tasks T1329 --output /tmp/test-single-export.json
   ```

3. Import into test project:
   ```bash
   cd /tmp/test-import-project
   cleo import-tasks /tmp/test-single-export.json
   ```

4. Verify import succeeded

## Expected Results
- New task created in test project with NEW ID (not T1329)
- Task title, description, status preserved
- Import logged in todo-log.json

## Verification
```bash
cd /tmp/test-import-project
cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks | length'  # Should be 1
cleo list --format json | jq '.tasks[0].title'  # Should match original
```

## Cleanup
```bash
rm -rf /tmp/test-import-project
```
before.blockedBy: scripts/export-tasks.sh lines 473-508 contain stub functions (build_export_package, validate_export_package, write_export_package) that override lib/export.sh implementations. Must remove stubs to use real library functions.

before.title: Integrate lib/export.sh into scripts/export-tasks.sh (remove stubs)
before.description:
## Objective
Remove stub functions from scripts/export-tasks.sh and integrate the real implementations from lib/export.sh.

## Root Cause
scripts/export-tasks.sh contains stub functions that shadow the complete implementations in lib/export.sh:
- build_export_package() at line 473-486 returns '{}'
- validate_export_package() at line 497-508 always returns success
- write_export_package() at line 520-544 is partial stub

## Required Changes
1. Add `source lib/export.sh` to the sourced libraries section
2. Remove the three stub function definitions from export-tasks.sh
3. Ensure the main() function calls the lib functions correctly
4. Test that export now produces valid packages

## Files to Modify
- scripts/export-tasks.sh (remove stubs, add source)
- Possibly lib/export.sh (verify function signatures match)

## Verification
```bash
# After fix, this should produce a valid package
bash scripts/export-tasks.sh T1329 --output /tmp/test.json
jq '._meta.format' /tmp/test.json  # Should be 'cleo-export'
jq '.tasks | length' /tmp/test.json  # Should be 1
```

## Unblocks
- T1337: Functional test: Export subtree
- T1338: Functional test: Import to different project

before.title: HITL: Human validation of export/import functionality
before.description:
## Objective
Human-in-the-loop validation that export/import functionality works end-to-end.

## IMPORTANT
**This task CANNOT be completed by an AI agent.** It requires explicit human confirmation that the export/import workflow functions correctly in a real-world scenario.

## Human Test Procedure
1. Export a real task or epic from the cleo project:
   ```bash
   cleo export-tasks <task-id> --subtree --output ~/test-export.json
   ```

2. Create a fresh test project:
   ```bash
   mkdir ~/test-import-project && cd ~/test-import-project
   cleo init
   ```

3. Import the exported tasks:
   ```bash
   cleo import-tasks ~/test-export.json
   ```

4. Verify:
   - Tasks appear with correct titles/descriptions
   - Hierarchy preserved (parent/child relationships)
   - Dependencies preserved
   - No data corruption

5. Clean up:
   ```bash
   rm -rf ~/test-import-project ~/test-export.json
   ```

## Completion Criteria
Human must explicitly confirm:
- [ ] Export creates valid JSON package
- [ ] Import successfully creates tasks
- [ ] Task data is preserved correctly
- [ ] No errors or data loss observed

## To Complete This Task
Run: `ct done T1351 --notes "HITL CONFIRMED: [your observations]"`

Only mark complete after personally verifying the workflow.

before.title: EPIC: Investigate Multi-Session Architecture Gap - Session Isolation Broken
before.description:
CRITICAL INVESTIGATION: Multi-session isolation is NOT working as expected.

## Problem Statement
Despite previous epics claiming multi-session was implemented (T1041, multi-session spec), the actual session isolation is BROKEN:
1. .current-session is a SINGLE file per project - last session start wins for ALL terminals
2. context-monitor.sh writes to .context-state.json (single file), not per-session files
3. context-alert.sh expects .context-state-{sessionId}.json files that aren't being written
4. Claude Code provides NO session ID ($CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is empty)
5. No terminal binding exists - nothing ties a terminal/Claude instance to a cleo session

## Evidence
- 10 terminals running claude + cleo would ALL share same .current-session
- Context state files exist per-session but statusline writes to wrong file
- Session registry (sessions.json) exists but binding mechanism is broken

## Investigation Required
1. Map COMPLETE session flow: start → bind → track → end
2. Trace where session registry (sessions.json) fits in
3. Identify what previous epics ACTUALLY implemented vs claimed
4. Document the gap between design and implementation
5. Determine if Claude Code CAN provide session/conversation ID
6. Design proper terminal-to-session binding mechanism

## Files to Investigate
- lib/sessions.sh - session registry functions
- scripts/session.sh - session commands
- lib/context-monitor.sh - statusline context tracking  
- lib/context-alert.sh - alert session detection
- .cleo/sessions.json - session registry
- specs/MULTI-SESSION-SPEC.md - original design

## Previous Related Tasks
- Multi-session epic (find and reference)
- Session scoping implementation
- Context safeguard system (T1320)

## Expected Output
- docs/research/session-architecture-investigation.md with complete flow diagram
- List of gaps between spec and implementation
- Recommended fixes prioritized by impact

after.title: EPIC: Investigate Multi-Session Architecture Gap - Session Isolation Broken
after.description:
CRITICAL INVESTIGATION: Multi-session isolation is NOT working as expected.

## Problem Statement
Despite previous epics claiming multi-session was implemented (T1041, multi-session spec), the actual session isolation is BROKEN:
1. .current-session is a SINGLE file per project - last session start wins for ALL terminals
2. context-monitor.sh writes to .context-state.json (single file), not per-session files
3. context-alert.sh expects .context-state-{sessionId}.json files that aren't being written
4. Claude Code provides NO session ID ($CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is empty)
5. No terminal binding exists - nothing ties a terminal/Claude instance to a cleo session

## Evidence
- 10 terminals running claude + cleo would ALL share same .current-session
- Context state files exist per-session but statusline writes to wrong file
- Session registry (sessions.json) exists but binding mechanism is broken

## Investigation Required
1. Map COMPLETE session flow: start → bind → track → end
2. Trace where session registry (sessions.json) fits in
3. Identify what previous epics ACTUALLY implemented vs claimed
4. Document the gap between design and implementation
5. Determine if Claude Code CAN provide session/conversation ID

before.title: Research: Investigate context-monitor.sh file write mismatch
before.description:
The statusline context monitor writes to WRONG file.

## Current Behavior
- context-monitor.sh writes to: .context-state.json (single file)
- context-alert.sh reads from: .context-state-{sessionId}.json

## Evidence
Many .context-state-{sessionId}.json files exist, so SOMETHING writes them.
But statusline writes to .context-state.json (no session ID).

## Investigate
1. What CREATES the per-session context state files?
2. Is there duplicate code paths?
3. Was context-monitor.sh updated when session support was added?
4. Does CLAUDE_SESSION_ID ever get set?

## Key Issue
Statusline runs OUTSIDE cleo commands - how can it know which session is active?
This is a fundamental architecture question.

before.title: Design: Session binding that supports cross-terminal resume
before.description:
Design session binding mechanism that supports BOTH:
1. Cross-terminal resume (session starts T1, resumes T2)
2. Concurrent sessions in same project (T1 on epic A, T2 on epic B)

## Constraint
Sessions MUST be able to cross terminals when Claude context fills up.
User closes Terminal 1, opens Terminal 2, runs 'cleo session resume <id>'.

## Challenge
.current-session approach works for cross-terminal (shared file)
But it BREAKS for concurrent sessions (last writer wins)

## Options to Evaluate
A. Environment variable from Claude Code (ideal but unavailable)
B. Terminal identifier binding (PPID/TTY - unreliable)
C. Explicit --session flag on every command (bad UX)
D. Session lease/lock mechanism (complex)
E. Single-active limitation (simplest but limiting)

## Deliverable
Design doc with recommended approach, trade-offs, implementation plan

before.title: Fix: Remove 2>/dev/null from context alert calls in all scripts
before.description:
IMMEDIATE FIX: Context alerts are being suppressed.

## Bug
All 4 scripts have:
```bash
check_context_alert 2>/dev/null || true
```

The 2>/dev/null suppresses stderr where alerts are written\!

## Fix
Change to:
```bash
check_context_alert || true
```

## Files to Fix
- scripts/add-task.sh (line ~1419)
- scripts/complete-task.sh
- scripts/focus.sh
- scripts/session.sh (2 places)

## Note
This is separate from the session isolation issue.
Even with isolation fixed, alerts won't show if stderr is suppressed.

before.title: Implement: lib/injection.sh - Core injection functions
before.description: Create lib/injection.sh as Layer 2 library sourcing injection-registry.sh and atomic-write.sh. Pure functions: extract_injection_version(file) → version string, validate_injection_markers(file) → exit code, check_injection_status(file) → missing|legacy|current|outdated. Side-effect functions: inject_template(file) → adds injection, update_injection(file) → replaces injection, strip_injection_block(file) → removes injection. All use atomic file operations.

before.title: IMPLEMENTATION: Create lib/json-output.sh library
before.description: Implement centralized JSON output library following JSON-OUTPUT-LIBRARY-SPEC.md. Create lib/json-output.sh with 6 core functions: output_json_envelope, output_json_compact, output_json_array, output_json_paginated, sort_json_array, limit_json_array. Must use jq -c for all outputs (compact mode). Include source guard, proper documentation, error handling. Add to lib/ directory structure. Reference implementation: lib/error-json.sh (lines 142-177). Will have 3 subtasks for function groups.

before.title: Implement: lib/injection.sh - Core injection functions
before.description: Create lib/injection.sh as Layer 2 library sourcing injection-registry.sh and atomic-write.sh. Pure functions: extract_injection_version(file) → version string, validate_injection_markers(file) → exit code, check_injection_status(file) → missing|legacy|current|outdated. Side-effect functions: inject_template(file) → adds injection, update_injection(file) → replaces injection, strip_injection_block(file) → removes injection. All use atomic file operations.

after.title: Implement: lib/injection.sh - Core injection functions
after.description: Create lib/injection.sh as Layer 2 library sourcing injection-registry.sh and atomic-write.sh. Pure functions: extract_injection_version(file) → version string, validate_injection_markers(file) → exit code, check_injection_status(file) → missing|legacy|current|outdated. Side-effect functions: inject_template(file) → adds injection, update_injection(file) → replaces injection, strip_injection_block(file) → removes injection. All use atomic file operations.

before.title: Implement: lib/injection.sh - Batch operations
before.description: Add batch operation functions to lib/injection.sh: check_all_injections() → JSON report of all injectable files status, update_all_injections() → updates all outdated files, get_injection_summary() → compact status for upgrade --status. Enables cleo upgrade to report on all doc files, not just CLAUDE.md.

after.title: Implement: lib/injection.sh - Batch operations
after.description: Add batch operation functions to lib/injection.sh: check_all_injections() → JSON report of all injectable files status, update_all_injections() → updates all outdated files, get_injection_summary() → compact status for upgrade --status. details.note: T1384 session: Discovered fundamental architecture misunderstanding - should remove --update-claude-md flag entirely. CLEO must be agent-agnostic. Commands: init (new projects only), upgrade (auto-update injections), validate --fix, install --global, doctor. Created T1459 bug report. Root cause: injection_update_all() line 185 captures stderr trace instead of JSON. CRITICAL: Must fix T1459 before continuing with T1396/T1397/T1398.

before.title: DEBUG: init.sh injection not creating agent files
before.description: CRITICAL: After implementing injection in init.sh (T1396), agent files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md) NOT created during init. Silent failure - no errors, no files, no log output. Code exists at scripts/init.sh:707-718. Testing: bash -c 'cd /tmp/test && cleo init' creates .cleo/ but no .md files. Possible causes: (1) injection.sh not sourced, (2) injection_update_all() failing silently, (3) jq parsing error, (4) CLEO_HOME unset, (5) FORMAT=json suppressing logs. Dependencies verified: all lib files exist, syntax OK. Installed via install.sh. Blocks T1400, T1402, T1384 completion. before.title: EPIC: Automated Schema Migration System with Pre-Commit Hooks
before.description: Implement automated schema migration generation to prevent manual workflow bypasses and agent confusion. Three-layer defense: (1) Remove migrate command from agent docs - agents only see 'cleo upgrade', (2) Add developer mode gate to migrate command with interactive warning/redirect, (3) Create intelligent pre-commit hook that auto-generates migration functions by analyzing schema diffs. Includes smart diff analyzer that classifies changes (PATCH/MINOR/MAJOR) and generates appropriate code - PATCH changes fully automated, MINOR/MAJOR get templates with TODOs. Replaces manual 'cleo migrate create' workflow. Prevents T1462 class of bugs where agents bypass automation.

before.title: Implement: cleo doctor command
before.description: Implement diagnostic command (aligned with Claude Code 'claude doctor'): 1) Check global agent configs exist and have @ reference, 2) Check project-level injection status, 3) Validate CLEO installation integrity, 4) Report missing/outdated components, 5) Suggest fixes (run 'cleo install --global' or 'cleo upgrade'). after.title: Implement: cleo doctor command
after.description: Implement COMPREHENSIVE diagnostic command (aligned with Claude Code pattern). SCOPE: Full health check from global ~/.cleo/ → all registered projects. NEW REQUIREMENT: Read ~/.cleo/projects-registry.json to discover and validate all projects. Use existing tools (validate.sh, upgrade --status, context) for DRY. Checks: (1) Global CLEO health (version, docs, configs), (2) Agent config status, (3) All registered projects (schema versions, integrity, injection status), (4) Overall ecosystem health score. Graduated exit codes (0/50/51/52/100). Support --fix for auto-repair. Output: JSON + text with actionable fixes.

before.title: EPIC: TODO_Task_Management.md SOP Compliance Optimization
before.description: Optimize TODO_Task_Management.md (4,174 words, ~5,426 tokens) to comply with CLEO-DOCUMENTATION-SOP.md v2.0.0. Current compliance: 60% (PARTIAL). Target: 80% compliance, 40% token reduction (5,426 → 3,256 tokens). Key improvements: (1) Apply RFC 2119 keywords to 100+ statements (currently 4). (2) Consolidate 49 code examples to 15-20. before.title: Investigate: Project registry not persisting after upgrade
before.description: After deploying v0.50.3 fixes, ~/.cleo/projects-registry.json is missing again despite appearing to be created during upgrade. Symptoms: (1) Registry file existed briefly at 08:12, (2) Now missing after subsequent operations, (3) upgrade.sh update_project_registration() may have logic issues. Need deep investigation of: when registry is created vs deleted, race conditions, error paths that silently fail, whether backup/restore operations affect registry. This is a regression from the T1520 fix.

after.title: Investigate: Project registry not persisting after upgrade
after.description: After deploying v0.50.3 fixes, ~/.cleo/projects-registry.json is missing again despite appearing to be created during upgrade. Symptoms: (1) Registry file existed briefly at 08:12, (2) Now missing after subsequent operations, (3) upgrade.sh update_project_registration() may have logic issues. Need deep investigation of: when registry is created vs deleted, race conditions, error paths that silently fail, whether backup/restore operations affect registry. This is a regression from the T1520 fix.

after.description: Research, design, and implement a comprehensive recipes system for canned workflows that enables LLM agents and users to execute complex multi-step project management workflows via single CLI commands. Integrates with CLEO position system (T805), verification gates (T1150), orchestration platform (T1062), roadmap generation (T1165), git workflow (T1114), and multi-file injection (T1384). Focus on agent-first design, security, parallel execution, and cross-epic coordination. Deliverable: claudedocs/specs/RECIPES-SYSTEM-SPEC.md for planning phase.

before.title: Epic: CLEO Orchestration Platform - Deterministic State Machine for LLM Agent Workflows
before.description: Transform CLEO from a task management CLI into a full CI/CD-like orchestration platform for LLM agent workflows. Implements deterministic Python-based routing (not LLM interpretation), verification gates for subagent coordination via DuckDB state, and templated prompts per agent role. Covers all 5 pipeline layers: Ingestion (60-69), RCSD (30-39), Implementation (40-49), Release (50-59), and Maintenance. Key principles: No API keys, deterministic routing, state in database, templated prompts, verification gates, session isolation, async where possible, hybrid polling/events. See claudedocs/CLEO-ORCHESTRATION-PLATFORM-PROPOSAL.md for full proposal.

after.title: Epic: CLEO Orchestration Platform - Deterministic State Machine for LLM Agent Workflows

Implements deterministic Python-based routing (not LLM interpretation), verification gates for subagent coordination via DuckDB state, and templated prompts per agent role. Covers all 5 pipeline layers: Ingestion (60-69), RCSD (30-39), Implementation (40-49), Release (50-59), and Maintenance. Key principles: No API keys, deterministic routing, state in database, templated prompts, verification gates, session isolation, async where possible, hybrid polling/events. See claudedocs/CLEO-ORCHESTRATION-PLATFORM-PROPOSAL.md for full proposal.

before.title: Add duplicate ID prevention to archive command

after.title: Add duplicate ID prevention to archive command

before.title: Implement validate --fix-duplicates for data repair
before.description: Extend validate command with --fix-duplicates flag that: (1) Detects all duplicate ID scenarios, (2) Provides interactive resolution (rename archived copy, delete duplicate, merge), (3) Updates sequence counter after repair, (4) Creates backup before any changes. Must handle: duplicate in archive, same ID in both files, sequence counter behind max ID.

before.title: Orchestrator Protocol Implementation
before.description: Implement a comprehensive Orchestrator Protocol for AI agent coordination. The system enforces: (1) immutable repeatable core instructions for every conversation, (2) context rot protection by keeping orchestrator high-level, (3) subagent-only execution where orchestrator NEVER does detailed work, (4) CLEO task management for tracking all work via Epics/Tasks/Subtasks with dependencies, (5) manifest-based handoff for subagent research sharing via MANIFEST.jsonl linked to tasks, (6) serial dependency execution preventing overlapping agents unless work is truly parallel, (7) autonomous operation running without user prompts after initial request.

before.title: Convert EPIC-ARCHITECT to Skill Format
before.description:
WHAT: Create skills/epic-architect/SKILL.md with proper YAML frontmatter (name, description, version, model). Extract core epic creation workflow from templates/orchestrator-protocol/subagent-prompts/EPIC-ARCHITECT.md (773 lines). Create progressive disclosure structure where SKILL.md body contains essential instructions (~1500-2000 words) and detailed content goes to references/.

WHY: Skills load ON-DEMAND via Skill tool (unlike CLAUDE.md which loads ALWAYS). Per skill-system-research, subagents do NOT inherit parent session skills - this prevents orchestrator constraints from polluting subagent contexts.

WHERE:
- Source: templates/orchestrator-protocol/subagent-prompts/EPIC-ARCHITECT.md (v2.1.0)
- Target: skills/epic-architect/SKILL.md
- Reference: skills/orchestrator/SKILL.md (use as structure template)

HOW:
1. Create skills/epic-architect/ directory
2. Create SKILL.md with frontmatter: name: epic-architect, description with trigger phrases ('create epic', 'plan epic', 'decompose into tasks', 'architect work', 'break down project', 'epic planning'), version: 2.1.0, model: sonnet
3. Extract essential content for SKILL.md body (Epic Structure, CLEO Commands, Task Decomposition, Wave Planning)
4. Keep detailed patterns (Research, Bug, Brownfield) for references/

ACCEPTANCE:
- [ ] SKILL.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter
- [ ] Description includes 5+ trigger phrases in quotes
- [ ] Body under 500 lines / 3000 words (per skill-system-research guidelines)
- [ ] Skill tool can load it successfully
- [ ] Progressive disclosure structure follows skill-system-research patterns

REFERENCES:
- skills/orchestrator/SKILL.md (v1.0.0 as template)
- claudedocs/research-outputs/2026-01-18_skill-system-research.md (structure requirements)
- templates/orchestrator-protocol/subagent-prompts/EPIC-ARCHITECT.md (source content)

before.title: Fix: Remove 2>/dev/null from context alert calls in all scripts
before.description:
IMMEDIATE FIX: Context alerts are being suppressed.

## Bug
All 4 scripts have:
```bash
check_context_alert 2>/dev/null || true
```

The 2>/dev/null suppresses stderr where alerts are written\!

## Fix
Change to:
```bash
check_context_alert || true
```

## Files to Fix
- scripts/add-task.sh (line ~1419)
- scripts/complete-task.sh
- scripts/focus.sh
- scripts/session.sh (2 places)

## Note
This is separate from the session isolation issue.
Even with isolation fixed, alerts won't show if stderr is suppressed.

before.title: EPIC: Session System Documentation & Enhancement
before.description:
EPIC: Session System Documentation & Enhancement

**RCSD Phases:**

## Phase 1: Research & Design (setup)
- T1013: Design seamless multi-agent session binding [CRITICAL BLOCKER]

## Phase 2: Core Fixes (core) 
Fix bugs blocking proper multi-session usage:
- T1012: Auto-set currentSessionId in sessions.json
- T1008: Fix session status to read from sessions.json
- T1011: Default multiSession.enabled=true in templates
- T1010: Clean up stale migrated legacy sessions

## Phase 3: Validation & UX (core)
- T1003: Improve session start UX when multiSession disabled
- T1004: Validate agent ID uniqueness
- T1005: Validate scope type enum

## Phase 4: Documentation (polish)
- T999: Rewrite docs/commands/session.md
- T1000: Document scope types and conflict detection
- T1001: Document sessions.json file structure
- T1002: Document multi-session configuration
- T1007: Update TODO_Task_Management.md
- T1009: Document CLEO_SESSION requirement

## Phase 5: Enhancements (polish)
- T1006: Auto-generate session names from scope

**Execution Model:**
- Each phase = 1 scoped session
- Tasks within phase can be parallelized by multiple agents
- Phase N+1 blocked until Phase N complete

before.title: EPIC: RCSD Python Agent Implementation (Anthropic Agent SDK)
before.description:
Master epic for Python-based RCSD Pipeline implementation using Anthropic Agent SDK. Supersedes bash-script approach in T719 children. Implements 9-agent orchestration (1 Research + 5 Consensus Workers + 1 Synthesis + 1 Spec + 1 Decompose) as Python package under lib/rcsd/ callable from bash CLI wrappers.

## Architecture Overview
- Python package: lib/rcsd/
- Entry point: python -m rcsd.cli <stage> <args>
- Bash wrappers: scripts/{research,consensus,spec,decompose}.sh
- Output: LLM-AGENT-FIRST compliant JSON to stdout

## Agent → subagent_type Mapping
| RCSD Agent | subagent_type |
|------------|---------------|
| Research | deep-research-agent |
| Technical Validator | backend-architect |
| Design Philosophy | frontend-architect |
| Documentation | technical-writer |
| Implementation | refactoring-expert |
| Challenge/Red Team | requirements-analyst |
| Synthesis | project-supervisor-orchestrator |
| Spec Writer | technical-writer |
| Decompose | requirements-analyst |

## Exit Codes (30-39 reserved)
EXIT_RESEARCH_FAILED=30, EXIT_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES=31, EXIT_CONSENSUS_FAILED=32, EXIT_HITL_REQUIRED=33, EXIT_SPEC_INVALID=34, EXIT_ATOMICITY_FAILED=35

## Key Specs
- RCSD-PIPELINE-SPEC.md (v1.0.0 authoritative)
- LLM-AGENT-FIRST-SPEC.md (JSON output)
- CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (consensus protocol)
- TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC.md (decompose stage)

before.title: HITL Testing: Validate automated migration system
before.description: Human-in-the-loop validation of complete automated migration system. Test scenarios: (1) PATCH change: Edit schema with maxLength increase, commit, verify hook generates complete migration automatically. (2) MINOR change: Add new optional field to schema, commit, verify hook generates template with correct TODO structure. (3) MAJOR change: Make breaking change, verify hook generates warning template. (4) Developer mode: Test CLEO_DEVELOPER_MODE=1 bypass of migrate gate. (5) Agent protection: Verify migrate command shows warning interactively, allows status/check without warning. (6) Documentation: Confirm agents cannot learn migrate command from docs. Success criteria: All scenarios work as designed, no false positives, clean user experience. BLOCKS: Epic T1463 completion.

before.title: NEXUS Agent Protocol Specification
before.description: Define the NEXUS Agent Protocol - a specification for HOW LLM agents behave and communicate. NEXUS is the agent behavior layer, while CLEO handles WHERE agents run (tmux orchestration). Separation of concerns: NEXUS = agent protocol/behavior, CLEO = infrastructure/state management. This epic covers: agent communication patterns, event-based handoffs, schema ownership model, verification gate protocols, and integration with CLEO's tmux orchestration.

after.title: NEXUS Agent Protocol Specification
after.description: Define the NEXUS Agent Protocol - a specification for HOW LLM agents behave and communicate. NEXUS is the agent behavior layer, while CLEO handles WHERE agents run (tmux orchestration). Separation of concerns: NEXUS = agent protocol/behavior, CLEO = infrastructure/state management. This epic covers: agent communication patterns, event-based handoffs, schema ownership model, verification gate protocols, and integration with CLEO's tmux orchestration.

before.title: Workflow Recipes System Integration
before.description: Research, design, and implement a comprehensive recipes system for canned workflows that enables LLM agents and users to execute complex multi-step project management workflows via single CLI commands. Integrates with CLEO position system (T805), verification gates (T1150), orchestration platform (T1062), roadmap generation (T1165), git workflow (T1114), and multi-file injection (T1384). Focus on agent-first design, security, parallel execution, and cross-epic coordination. Deliverable: claudedocs/specs/RECIPES-SYSTEM-SPEC.md for planning phase.

Fix 80 failing tests discovered after CI infrastructure repair. Tests now run but many fail.

## GitHub Issue
https://github.com/kryptobaseddev/cleo/issues/3

## Background
CI was failing immediately due to:
1. statusline-setup.sh set -e exit (fixed v0.48.4)
2. Wrong .claude/ directory in ci.yml (fixed)
3. Incorrect bats paths in test files (fixed)

Now tests run (2269+ total) but 80 fail.

- LLM-Agent-First compliance: 17 tests
- Phase sync: 17 tests  
- Analyze size strategy: 11 tests
- Config system: 10 tests
- Hierarchy auto-complete: 7 tests
- TodoWrite sync: 7 tests
- Upgrade command: 6 tests
- Other (backup, error codes): 5 tests

## Last Successful CI
v0.30.1 on Dec 23, 2025

before.title: Enhance discover_related_tasks() with hierarchy method and boosting
before.description:
Integrate _discover_by_hierarchy() into the public discover_related_tasks() API and add hierarchical boosting to the 'auto' method.

**Changes Required**:

1. **Add 'hierarchy' case** to discover_related_tasks():
```bash
case "$method" in
    ...
    hierarchy)
        _discover_by_hierarchy "$task_id"
        ;;
```

2. **Update 'auto' method** to include hierarchy results:
```bash
auto|all)
    local labels_result description_result files_result hierarchy_result
    labels_result=$(_discover_by_labels "$task_id")
    description_result=$(_discover_by_description "$task_id")
    files_result=$(_discover_by_files "$task_id")
    hierarchy_result=$(_discover_by_hierarchy "$task_id")
    
    # Merge with hierarchy boosting applied
    echo "$labels_result" "$description_result" "$files_result" "$hierarchy_result" | \
        jq -s 'add | group_by(.taskId) | map(max_by(.score)) | sort_by(-.score)'
    ;;
```

3. **Add sibling/cousin boosting** to merged results:
- If task pair appears in hierarchy_result as sibling: add +0.2 to final score
- If task pair appears in hierarchy_result as cousin: add +0.1 to final score

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- 'hierarchy' method works standalone
- 'auto' method includes hierarchy in combined results
- Sibling boost (+0.2) applied correctly
- Cousin boost (+0.1) applied correctly
- Backward compatible (existing methods unchanged)
- Help text updated to include 'hierarchy' method

before.title: Implement _find_lca() function for lowest common ancestor
before.description:
Implement _find_lca() function to find the lowest common ancestor of two tasks in the hierarchy.

**Function Signature**:
```bash
_find_lca() {
    local task_a="$1"
    local task_b="$2"
    # Returns: LCA task ID or empty string if no common ancestor
}
```

**Algorithm**:
1. Build ancestor path for task_a (task -> parent -> grandparent -> root)
2. Build ancestor path for task_b
3. Find first common element (LCA)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- Returns correct LCA for siblings (parent)
- Returns correct LCA for cousins (grandparent)
- Returns empty string for unrelated tasks (different root)
- Handles edge cases: same task input, null parentId
- Uses existing _graph_rag_get_task() utility

before.title: EPIC: Hierarchical Discovery System for graph-rag.sh
before.description:
Implement PageIndex-inspired hierarchical discovery features in lib/graph-rag.sh to improve task relationship discovery accuracy by 15-20%.

**Research Reference**: claudedocs/research-outputs/pageindex-rag-research.md (T2153)

**Phase 1 Scope (v0.69.0)**:
- Add _discover_by_hierarchy() function
- Add _get_hierarchical_context() for parent context propagation  
- Add _find_lca() for lowest common ancestor calculation
- Add _tree_distance() for hierarchy distance
- Enhance discover_related_tasks() with hierarchy method
- Add sibling/cousin relationship boosting

**Success Criteria**:
- +15-20% accuracy improvement on sibling task relationships
- Negligible performance impact (uses existing parentId field)
- Backward compatible with existing relates schema
- Unit tests in tests/unit/graph-rag-hierarchy.bats

**Deliverables**:
1. lib/graph-rag.sh updates (~150 lines)
2. tests/unit/graph-rag-hierarchy.bats
3. docs/commands/graph-rag.md updates

before.title: Session A: GSD Pattern Extraction & CLEO Integration
before.description:
Research stream from orchestrator session focused on GSD pattern extraction and CLEO integration planning.

## Session Identifier
**Session A** - Orchestrator-coordinated multi-agent research session

## Research Scope
1. GSD codebase mapping analysis
2. GSD atomic commit discipline
3. GSD phase-flow UX patterns
4. CLEO RCSD specs audit
5. Consensus building (5 questions)
6. Validation against CLEO constraints

## Research Outputs (6 files)
- 2026-01-23_gsd-codebase-mapping-analysis.md
- 2026-01-23_gsd-atomic-commit-analysis.md
- 2026-01-23_gsd-phase-flow-analysis.md
- 2026-01-23_cleo-rcsd-specs-audit.md
- 2026-01-23_gsd-cleo-consensus-report.md
- 2026-01-23_consensus-validation-report.md

## Key Decisions Made
1. Single .cleo/codebase/codebase-map.json (not split files)
2. Warn-only staleness with opt-in auto-refresh
3. Auto-create tasks with dry-run preview required
4. Per-task changesets with multi-commit array
5. Implicit workflowState metadata (no phase commands)

## Conflicts Flagged
- TBD: Awaiting input from other sessions to identify conflicts

Aggregate all conflicts identified across sessions and prepare for resolution.

## Purpose
After all session task groups are complete, compile:
1. All conflicts flagged in session notes
2. Decision divergences across sessions
3. Conflicting evidence/rationale

## Deliverable
`claudedocs/research-outputs/CONFLICT-MATRIX.md` containing:

| Question | Session A | Session B | Session C | Resolution Needed |
|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-------------------|
| ... | ... | ... | ... | Yes/No |

## Blocked By
All session tasks (consensus-source label) must be complete.

## Triggers
T2208 (Final Synthesis) once conflicts are documented.

before.title: Final Consensus Synthesis
before.description:
Review ALL research across ALL sessions and produce the authoritative consensus.

## Inputs
1. All session task groups (label: consensus-source)
2. Conflict matrix (T2207)
3. All linked research files

## Process
1. Read all session research outputs
2. Compare decisions across sessions
3. For each conflict: apply structured debate
4. Document resolution rationale
5. Produce unified consensus

## Deliverables

### 1. UNIFIED-CONSENSUS.md
Final decisions on all key questions with:
- Decision statement
- Which sessions agreed
- How conflicts were resolved
- Implementation implications

### 2. Implementation Epic Spec
Ready-to-create epic with tasks following consensus decisions.
No ambiguity. No assumptions. Proven patterns only.

## Blocked By
- T2207 (Conflict Matrix) must be complete
- All consensus-source tasks must be complete

## Success Criteria
- All conflicts resolved with documented rationale
- Single authoritative decision per question
- No contradictions in final document
- Implementation epic can proceed without further debate

before.title: Session B: Multi-Agent GSD Deep Research & Consensus Validation
before.description:
Research stream using 4 parallel deep-research subagents to analyze GSD patterns.

## Session Identifier
**Session B** - Multi-agent orchestrated research (4 parallel agents)

## Research Scope
1. GSD codebase mapping (gsd-codebase-mapper agent, templates)
2. GSD atomic commit discipline (execute-plan, git-integration)
3. GSD deviation/HITL patterns (checkpoints, verification)
4. CLEO RCSD gap analysis (specs audit)

## Research Method
Spawned 4 parallel deep-research-agent subagents analyzing:
- Agent 1: Codebase Mapping Analysis
- Agent 2: Atomic Commit & Change Tracking
- Agent 3: Deviation Rules & HITL
- Agent 4: CLEO RCSD Gap Analysis

## Key Decisions (5 Questions)
1. Codebase Map: Single JSON - ✅ AGREES with Session A
2. Staleness: Warn-only default - ✅ AGREES with Session A
3. Task Creation: Auto w/ dry-run - ✅ AGREES with Session A
4. Changesets: Per-task w/ commits[] - ✅ AGREES with Session A
5. Phase UX: Implicit states - ✅ AGREES with Session A

## Conflicts
**NONE** - All 5 decisions align with Session A.

## Value Added
- Line-number citations from GSD source files
- 4-rule deviation model → CLEO consensus mapping
- Checkpoint distribution (90/9/1)
- Dynamic auth gate pattern
- GSD Golden Rule documentation

before.title: Delete unused injection_extract_version function
before.description:
**Problem**: `injection_extract_version()` in lib/injection-config.sh (lines 51-64) is dead code. It was designed for legacy versioned markers (`CLEO:START v1.0.0`) but:
1. New markers don't have versions
2. No code calls this function (verified via grep)
3. Function comment says 'returns empty for new versionless markers (which is expected)'

**Location**: lib/injection-config.sh lines 51-64

**Fix**: Delete the entire `injection_extract_version()` function.

**Dependency**: Should be done AFTER T2225 and T2226 (to confirm version field removal is complete)

**Acceptance**:
- [ ] Function removed from lib/injection-config.sh
- [ ] No grep hits for injection_extract_version in codebase
- [ ] All tests still pass
- [ ] Documentation updated if function was documented

before.title: Sync features from config.json during upgrade
before.description:
**Problem**: DRY violation - init.sh (lines 946-954) and upgrade.sh (lines 446-451) hardcode identical feature defaults:
```json
"features": {
    "multiSession": false,
    "verification": false,
    "contextAlerts": false
}
```
These should read actual values from config.json instead of hardcoding.

**Locations**:
- scripts/init.sh lines 946-954
- scripts/upgrade.sh lines 446-451

**Fix**: Read feature settings from config.json:
```bash
local multi_session=$(jq -r '.multiSession.enabled // false' "$config_file")
local verification=$(jq -r '.verification.enabled // false' "$config_file")
local context_alerts=$(jq -r '.contextAlerts.enabled // false' "$config_file")
```

Then use these values in the jq expression.

**Dependency**: Requires T2228 (schema update) to be complete first

**Acceptance**:
- [ ] init.sh reads features from config.json
- [ ] upgrade.sh reads features from config.json
- [ ] New projects get default values (no config.json yet)
- [ ] Existing projects reflect actual config settings
- [ ] DRY principle satisfied - no duplicate hardcoded values

before.title: Add feature consistency health check to doctor
before.description:
**Problem**: Doctor doesn't validate that features in project-info.json match config.json settings. This means stale project-info.json files can have incorrect feature flags.

**Current behavior**: Doctor checks validation, schema versions, injection status - but NOT feature consistency.

**Fix**: Add new health check to doctor.sh:
1. Read features from project-info.json
2. Read actual settings from config.json
3. Compare and warn if mismatched
4. Issue code: FEATURE_MISMATCH
5. Suggested fix: `cleo upgrade`

**Example output**:
```
⚠ Feature mismatch: multiSession is false in project-info but enabled in config
  Fix: Run 'cleo upgrade' to sync feature status
```

**Dependency**: Requires T2228 (schema) and T2229 (sync logic) to be complete

**Acceptance**:
- [ ] Doctor detects feature mismatches
- [ ] Warning shown with specific mismatched features
- [ ] Fix command suggested
- [ ] No false positives (handles missing config gracefully)
- [ ] Check is skipped for new projects without config.json

before.title: Final Consensus Synthesis
before.description:
Review ALL research across ALL sessions and produce the authoritative consensus.

## Inputs
1. All session task groups (label: consensus-source)
2. Conflict matrix (T2207)
3. All linked research files

## Process
1. Read all session research outputs
2. Compare decisions across sessions
3. For each conflict: apply structured debate
4. Document resolution rationale
5. Produce unified consensus

## Deliverables

### 1. UNIFIED-CONSENSUS.md
Final decisions on all key questions with:

before.title: RCSD Integration Research Consolidation
before.description:
Meta-orchestration epic to consolidate all parallel RCSD/GSD research streams into a single validated consensus.

## Problem
Multiple agent sessions are conducting parallel research on:
- GSD codebase mapping patterns
- RCSD pipeline implementation
- Atomic commit discipline integration
- Phase-flow UX adaptation
- Consensus mechanisms

Different sessions may have divergent conclusions. This epic establishes a structured process to:
1. Collect all research under tagged task groups
2. Cross-validate findings
3. Resolve conflicts through structured debate
4. Produce a single authoritative consensus

## Protocol
Each contributing agent session MUST:
1. Create a child task group under this epic with session identifier
2. Link ALL their research outputs to their task group
3. Document key decisions and rationale
4. Explicitly flag conflicts with other sessions' findings

## Deliverables
- Tagged research from all sessions
- Cross-session conflict matrix
- Final unified consensus document
- Implementation roadmap with no ambiguity

## Success Criteria
- 100% research linked to task groups
- All conflicts identified and resolved
- Single consensus document approved
- Implementation epic ready for execution

before.title: Decision: Changeset Storage Model
before.description:
Decide where changeset data is stored.

## Options Identified
A. **Embedded in task** - Store in task.changeset field in todo.json
   - Pros: Simple, single file, CLEO-native
   - Cons: Bloats todo.json, limited metadata

B. **Separate files** - Store in .cleo/changes/T001.json per task
   - Pros: GSD pattern, rich metadata, reviewable, git-trackable
   - Cons: More files, more complexity

C. **@changesets/cli compatible** - Store in .changeset/*.md
   - Pros: Industry standard, external tooling support
   - Cons: Node dependency, different paradigm

before.title: Decision: Changeset Capture Timing
before.description:
Decide WHEN changeset data is captured.

## Options Identified
A. **On ct complete** - Automatic capture when task is marked done
   - Pros: Zero friction, guaranteed capture
   - Cons: May miss multi-commit work, timing issues

B. **Manual ct changeset** - Explicit command to capture changes
   - Pros: More control, developer decides what to include
   - Cons: More friction, may be forgotten

C. **Per-commit hook** - Git hook captures on each commit
   - Pros: Captures ALL commits, complete audit trail
   - Cons: Complex setup, may capture too much noise

D. **Hybrid** - Auto on complete + manual override
   - Pros: Best of both worlds
   - Cons: More complex implementation

before.title: Decision: Git Integration Depth
before.description:
Decide HOW DEEP git integration goes for changesets.

## Options Identified
A. **Shallow** - Just commit hash
   - Captured: hash only
   - Pros: Minimal, fast
   - Cons: Need git log to see details

B. **Medium** - Hash + file list
   - Captured: hash, list of files changed
   - Pros: Know what was touched without git
   - Cons: More storage, but manageable

C. **Deep** - Hash + files + diff summary
   - Captured: hash, files, insertions/deletions, maybe commit message
   - Pros: Complete audit trail, self-contained
   - Cons: Storage bloat, complexity

## TDD Consideration
With RED/GREEN/REFACTOR creating 2-3 commits per task, deeper capture means more data.

before.title: Decision: Commit Message Format Standard
before.description:
Decide the standard commit message format for CLEO workflows.

## Options Identified
A. **Conventional Commits** - {type}({scope}): {description}
   - Example: feat(auth): add JWT refresh token support
   - Pros: Industry standard, tooling support, semantic-release compatible
   - Cons: No task linkage built-in

B. **Phase-Plan Format** - {type}({phase}-{plan}): {description}
   - Example: feat(core-T1234): add JWT refresh token support
   - Pros: Explicit phase context, task linkage
   - Cons: Non-standard, no external tooling

C. **Hybrid** - Conventional + Task footer
   - Example: feat(auth): add JWT refresh token\n\nTask: T1234
   - Pros: Best of both, tooling compatible, task linked
   - Cons: Requires discipline to add footer

## Enforcement
Should CLEO validate commit messages? Hook? Advisory?

before.title: Decision: Release Automation Tooling
before.description:
Decide what tooling handles release automation.

## Options Identified
A. **release-please** (Google)
   - Reads conventional commits, creates Release PRs
   - Pros: Automated, creates PRs (branch protection friendly)
   - Cons: Another GitHub Action, opinionated changelog format

B. **@changesets/cli**
   - Developer creates .changeset files per PR
   - Pros: Explicit change tracking, great for monorepos
   - Cons: Node dependency, more workflow steps

C. **semantic-release**
   - Fully automated from commits
   - Pros: Zero manual intervention
   - Cons: Very opinionated, Node dependency

D. **Custom CLEO** (ct release commands)
   - Native CLEO commands: ct release plan/ship
   - Pros: CLEO-native, dogfooding, works for users too
   - Cons: Development effort, reinventing wheel?

E. **Hybrid** - CLEO + external tool
   - CLEO tracks changesets, external tool handles version/tag
   - Pros: Best of both
   - Cons: More moving parts

## For CLEO Dev vs For CLEO Users
Different answers may apply to CLEO development vs what CLEO offers its users.

## Session Contributions

## Current Status: AWAITING INPUT

before.title: Decision: RCSD → Implementation Handoff
before.description:
Define how RCSD Protocol outputs feed into Implementation Protocol.

## Handoff Points
1. Research findings → Task context
2. Consensus decisions → Implementation constraints
3. Specifications → Acceptance criteria
4. Decomposition → Epic/Task structure

## Questions to Resolve
- What artifacts does RCSD produce?
- How are they linked to implementation tasks?
- Is there a 'gate' between RCSD and Implementation?
- Can implementation start before full consensus?

## Phase-Flow UX Concepts
- 'Gray area' analysis before planning
- Wave-based parallel execution
- Goal-backward verification (task done ≠ goal achieved)
- Deviation rules: auto-fix bugs/critical/blocking, ask for architectural

before.title: Create CONTRIBUTION-PROTOCOL.template.md
before.description:
Create parameterized protocol template with token placeholders.

## Depends On
T2268 (pattern analysis) - need parameterization map

## Template Structure
1. **Header block** - `{{EPIC_ID}}`, `{{EPIC_TITLE}}`, `{{DATE}}`
2. **Baseline reference** - `{{BASELINE_SESSION_ID}}`, `{{BASELINE_DECISIONS}}`
3. **Decision questions** - `{{DECISION_QUESTIONS}}` (array injection)
4. **RFC 2119 block** - Numbered requirements (invariant)
5. **Validation checklist** - Agent self-check (invariant)

## Tokens Required
| Token | Type | Example |
|-------|------|---------|
| `{{EPIC_ID}}` | string | T2204 |
| `{{BASELINE_SESSION_ID}}` | string | T2205 |
| `{{DECISION_QUESTIONS}}` | array | [{id, question, baseline}] |

## Deliverable
`templates/CONTRIBUTION-PROTOCOL.template.md`

## Constraint
Total invariant content <500 tokens for low context impact

Create minimal injection block for subagent prompts.

T2268 (pattern analysis) - need RFC 2119 requirements list

## Constraint
**<200 tokens** - Must be injectable without significant context cost

## Content
1. RFC 2119 requirements (numbered, terse)
2. Output format requirement
3. Conflict flagging requirement
4. Completion criteria
5. Pointer to full protocol (not inline)

## Format
```markdown
## CONTRIBUTION PROTOCOL (RFC 2119)
[Numbered requirements here]
Full protocol: {{PROTOCOL_PATH}}
```

## Deliverable
`templates/CONTRIBUTION-INJECTION.md` (~150-200 tokens)

## Validation
- Token count verified
- All critical requirements present
- No redundancy with full protocol

before.title: Session C: Orchestrator Core Philosophy & Anti-Hallucination Protocol
before.description:
Research stream focused on orchestrator core philosophy, anti-hallucination patterns, and provenance enforcement.

## Session Identifier
**Session C** - HITL-driven brainstorm + 3 parallel subagent analysis

## Research Scope
1. LLM instruction stickiness and what makes instructions 'stick'
2. Anti-hallucination patterns and constraint optimization
3. Provenance enforcement mechanisms (commit, JSDoc, completion gates)
4. Deterministic agent contract specification

## Research Method
- Interactive HITL brainstorm session
- 3 parallel subagents: deep-research-agent, system-architect, requirements-analyst
- RFC 2119 specification development

## Key Decisions (Aligned with Sessions A & B + NEW)
1. **Core Mantra**: 'Scope down. Trace to Epic. No orphaned work.'
2. **Maximum Scope Limits**: 3 files/agent, 0 architectural decisions/agent
3. **JSDoc Standard**: @task (MUST), @epic (SHOULD), @why/@what (MUST)
4. **Enforcement Layers**: L1-Spawn (hard), L2-Commit (hard+audit), L3-Complete (soft), L4-JSDoc (progressive)
5. **Brownfield Strategy**: Zone-based (Green/Yellow/Red) with forward-only enforcement

## New Findings (Beyond Sessions A/B)
- LLM instruction stickiness research: parallelism, rhythm, concrete anchors
- Determinism equation: Determinism = (Atomicity × Provenance) / (Scope × Ambiguity)
- 4-layer enforcement architecture with escape hatches
- Agent contract schema with exit codes 60-69

## Conflicts with Sessions A/B
**NONE** - Builds upon and extends A/B findings. Adds enforcement specifics.

## Research Outputs
- 2026-01-25_llm-instruction-stickiness.md (from deep-research-agent)
- 2026-01-25_provenance-enforcement-design.md (from system-architect)
- 2026-01-25_deterministic-agent-contract.md (from requirements-analyst)
- docs/specs/AGENT-CONTRACT-SPEC.md (created by requirements-analyst)

before.title: Decision: Changeset Storage Model
before.description:
Decide where changeset data is stored.

## Options Identified
A. **Embedded in task** - Store in task.changeset field in todo.json
   - Pros: Simple, single file, CLEO-native
   - Cons: Bloats todo.json, limited metadata

B. **Separate files** - Store in .cleo/changes/T001.json per task
   - Pros: GSD pattern, rich metadata, reviewable, git-trackable
   - Cons: More files, more complexity

C. **@changesets/cli compatible** - Store in .changeset/*.md
   - Pros: Industry standard, external tooling support
   - Cons: Node dependency, different paradigm

before.title: Decision: Changeset Capture Timing
before.description:
Decide WHEN changeset data is captured.

## Options Identified
A. **On ct complete** - Automatic capture when task is marked done
   - Pros: Zero friction, guaranteed capture
   - Cons: May miss multi-commit work, timing issues

B. **Manual ct changeset** - Explicit command to capture changes
   - Pros: More control, developer decides what to include
   - Cons: More friction, may be forgotten

C. **Per-commit hook** - Git hook captures on each commit
   - Pros: Captures ALL commits, complete audit trail
   - Cons: Complex setup, may capture too much noise

D. **Hybrid** - Auto on complete + manual override
   - Pros: Best of both worlds
   - Cons: More complex implementation
